items.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
items.summary: AI inference startup Baseten is reportedly in the process of raising a massive $1.5 billion funding round, signaling strong investor confidence in the AI infrastructure market.

items.title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
items.summary: Elastic has reportedly agreed to acquire DeductiveAI for up to $85 million, indicating a strategic move to enhance its AI capabilities, particularly in search and data analysis.

items.title: Using AI to help physicians diagnose rare genetic diseases affecting children
items.summary: OpenAI is leveraging AI to develop tools that assist physicians in diagnosing rare genetic diseases in children, potentially accelerating critical medical interventions.

items.title: Snap spins off AI video team into new company, Dotmo, due to costs
items.summary: Snap has spun off its AI video team into a new independent company called Dotmo, citing high operational costs associated with advanced AI development.

items.title: Improving health intelligence in ChatGPT
items.summary: OpenAI is actively working on enhancing ChatGPT's health intelligence capabilities, aiming to provide more accurate and reliable information in medical contexts.

items.title: Beyond the Current Observation: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models in Controllable Non-Markov Games
items.summary: New research evaluates Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) in complex non-Markov games, pushing the boundaries of their decision-making and strategic capabilities beyond immediate observations.

items.title: New usage analytics and updated spend controls for enterprises
items.summary: OpenAI has introduced new usage analytics and updated spend controls for its enterprise clients, offering better oversight and management of AI resource consumption.

items.title: MolmoMotion: Forecasting Point Trajectories in 3D with Language Instruction
items.summary: MolmoMotion introduces a novel method for forecasting 3D point trajectories guided by natural language instructions, advancing the field of intuitive human-AI interaction for motion planning.

items.title: Kairos: A Native World Model Stack for Physical AI
items.summary: Kairos presents a native world model stack designed for physical AI systems, aiming to provide robots with a more robust and integrated understanding of their environment.

items.title: Tower Before Dusk: I Built a Puzzle Game for Humans and AI
items.summary: A developer shares their experience building a puzzle game designed to be challenging and engaging for both human players and AI agents, highlighting the intersection of game design and AI.

items.title: Our Competitor Had an AI That Covered 97.2%. We Had a Spreadsheet and a Fake Quote. Guess Who Won.
items.summary: An article humorously recounts a business scenario where a low-tech, human-driven approach triumphed over a competitor's advanced AI solution, emphasizing the importance of strategy over technology alone.

items.title: Guava: An Effective and Universal Harness for Embodied Manipulation
items.summary: Guava introduces a new, effective, and universal harness designed to facilitate embodied manipulation tasks, offering a standardized platform for robotic control and interaction.

items.title: EfficientRollout: System-Aware Self-Speculative Decoding for RL Rollouts
items.summary: EfficientRollout proposes a system-aware self-speculative decoding method to improve the efficiency of reinforcement learning rollouts, optimizing performance by anticipating future states.

items.title: The Reward Was in Your Data All Along: Correcting Flow Matching with Discriminator-Guided RL
items.summary: This research presents a novel approach to correct flow matching models by integrating discriminator-guided reinforcement learning, leveraging inherent data rewards for improved performance.

items.title: SAE Interventions are Unreliable: Post-Intervention Recovery of Suppressed Behavior
items.summary: A study reveals that SAE (Sparse Autoencoder) interventions in AI models can be unreliable, as suppressed behaviors may recover post-intervention, raising concerns about interpretability and control.
